name: Salvia Danshen Root
description: A valuable medicinal root in Eastern medicine. Rich in tanshinones and salvianolic acids that stimulate microvascular tissue circulation, promote blood flow, and speed up the healing of chronic skin ulcerations.
preparation: Microvascular Tea: Steep 5g of clean dried root in 300ml of hot water for 15 minutes. Strain and drink warm once daily.
safetyAlert: Well tolerated. Do not combine with modern anticoagulant or blood thinning pharmaceuticals.
interactions: Contraindicated with Warfarin, Aspirin, or heparin due to additive antiplatelet bleeding hazards.
